_doc: Registry of expert-prose source channels. The ETL (src/ingest/) is channel-agnostic and keys off this file. slug = the bucket name used in BOTH data/subs/<slug>/ (raw, gitignored) and draft-guides/<slug>/ (results, committed). fetch with: src/ingest/fetch_subs.sh <slug>; fingerprint with: src/ingest/fingerprint.py <slug>.
_distill_doc: distill_format below is the per-channel OUTPUT CONTRACT a stage-2 agent must satisfy when turning data/subs/<slug>/ transcripts into draft-guides/<slug>/ markdown. The shared 'recency-aware house style' (recency rule, Source timeline table, Supersessions, Source episodes list, (?) caveat for mangled auto-caption names) plus the step-by-step procedure live in src/ingest/DISTILL.md — channels here carry only the per-channel specifics.

lords-of-limited.name: Lords of Limited
lords-of-limited.notes: Channel #1 (original). Per-set consolidated guide; ## Card notes bullets are parsed into the draft JSON.

sections: ## Source timeline (dated table, Phase + Weight per episode)

card_bullets.heading: ## Card notes
card_bullets.parser_note: LOAD-BEARING: src/mtgdraft parses these bullets into each card's `guide` field in the draft JSON. Keep the exact `- **Card** — note` shape; card name must match the printed card name.

distill_format.recency: newest video supersedes older on conflict; prerelease/preview takes are weakest; the end-of-format retrospective is most authoritative

numot.name: NumotTheNummy (Kenji Egashira)
numot.notes: Channel #2. Gameplay-driven; auto-captions of speech (names mangled, mark (?)).

channel_files: general-tips.md (evergreen, set-independent draft & play principles, deduped and themed: picking, signals, curve/deckbuild, play patterns, mindset)
distill_format.recency: opinions surface as Kenji-while-drafting commentary; strongest signal on which cards are actually playable late
special_rules: Tier-1 sets (MKM, SOS) get a `## vs Lords of Limited` section noting ONLY genuine conflicts with draft-guides/lords-of-limited/<SET>-draft-guide.md. Never modify the LoL files.
Tier 1 = ALL VODs for that set (deepest); Tier 2 = ~4 end-of-format VODs per other set.

limited-resources.name: Limited Resources (Marshall Sutcliffe + Luis Scott-Vargas)
limited-resources.content_type: audio podcast on YouTube: card-by-card set reviews (letter grades) + format overviews
limited-resources.notes: Channel #3 (added 2026-06). Review/theory lens, decorrelated from the two gameplay channels; preserves LR's per-card letter grades. Audio auto-captions (names mangled, mark (?)).

sections: ### Source timeline (dated table, Phase release-week/early/mid/late/Sunset + Weight Weakest→Strongest)
## Supersessions (release-week grade flips: early take → late take, verdict)
## Archetypes (10 two-color pairs, ranked by the settled Sunset Show read)

card_bullets.heading: ## Card tips
card_bullets.format: - **Card** — grade (#NNN release). Late take if reversed.
card_bullets.grouping: by color W/U/B/R/G, then multicolor, then artifacts/lands
card_bullets.preserve: the LR letter grade inline on every card bullet — this is the channel's distinctive product

distill_format.recency: release-week set-review grades are PREDICTIONS made before any games (weakest evidence); the Sunset Show retrospective is the most authoritative source
special_rules: The Supersessions section (which release-week grades flipped, and by how much) is the single highest-leverage product of this channel — never omit it.

limited-level-ups.name: Limited Level-Ups (Alex / Chord_O_Calls)
limited-level-ups.content_type: Limited-focused podcast/video: per-color card-by-card set reviews (letter grades) + rare/mythic review + prerelease/first-impressions guides + occasional early-access draft gameplay + weekly strategy deep-dives
limited-level-ups.notes: Channel #4 (added 2026-06). Theory/review lens like limited-resources but split into per-color set-review episodes (W/U/B/R/G + Multicolor/Colorless + Rare/Mythic), so coverage is unusually deep per set. Preserves Alex's per-card letter grades. Audio auto-captions (names mangled, mark (?)).

sections: ### Source timeline (dated table, Phase prerelease/early/mid/late + Weight Weakest→Strongest)
## Supersessions (grade/opinion flips between first-impressions and later reviews)

card_bullets.heading: ## Card tips
card_bullets.grouping: by color W/U/B/R/G, then multicolor, then colorless/artifacts, then lands
card_bullets.preserve: Alex's per-card letter grade inline on every card bullet — this channel grades every card in dedicated per-color review episodes, so coverage should be near-complete

distill_format.recency: first-impressions/prerelease takes are predictions made before games (weakest); the per-color set reviews are the core graded product; early-access draft gameplay shows which cards actually played well
special_rules: This channel splits its set review across per-color episodes — distill ALL of them into one <SET>.md grouped by color, attributing each grade to its color-review episode.
Preserve the letter grade on every card bullet; coverage should be near-complete since every card gets reviewed.
